question: [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateReserve + PortalBase._setTokenReserve] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploiting the reserve uint128 truncation on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses a quote token where the reserve can exceed type(uint128).max (e.g. a low-value token with many decimals), and _setTokenReserve stores reserve as uint128 trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) buy tokens with a large amount of quote tokens such that the stored reserve exceeds type(uint128).max, (2) observe if the reserve is truncated when stored as uint128, (3) sell tokens using the truncated reserve, violating INVARIANT: stored reserve must equal the actual quote token balance held by Portal, causing scoped impact: if reserve is truncated, the curve uses a lower reserve than actual, allowing attacker to sell tokens for more quote tokens than the curve should allow? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test buying tokens until reserve > type(uint128).max, assert stored reserve == actual balance.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateSupply] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER buying tokens when the curve reserve is at the DEX threshold on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses CURVE_2 with DexThreshType.TWO_THIRDS (threshold = 6.67e8 ether), and circulatingSupply is just below the threshold trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) buy tokens such that estimateSupply(r + eth_in) crosses the DEX threshold, (2) observe if the buy is processed at the pre-threshold price but the token migrates to DEX mid-transaction, (3) check if the attacker receives tokens at the bonding curve price but the migration uses the post-buy reserve, violating INVARIANT: DEX migration must use the correct reserve and supply at the time of migration, causing scoped impact: BNB extraction if the migration uses a stale reserve that is higher than the actual reserve after fees? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test buying tokens to cross the DEX threshold, assert migration uses correct reserve.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateReserveV2] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploiting the (scaledReserve + scaleFactor - 1) / scaleFactor addition overflow on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses a 1-decimal quote token (scaleFactor=1e17), and scaledReserve is near type(uint256).max - scaleFactor + 1, causing scaledReserve + scaleFactor - 1 to overflow uint256 trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) sell tokens when supply is near 0 and curve has very large k, causing scaledReserve = divWadUp(k, TOTAL_SUPPLY + h - supply) - r to be near type(uint256).max, (2) observe if scaledReserve + scaleFactor - 1 overflows, violating INVARIANT: estimateReserveV2 must not overflow for any valid supply, causing scoped impact: if overflow wraps (impossible in 0.8+), attacker gets 0 quote tokens; if it reverts, DoS? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test with scaledReserve = type(uint256).max - 1e17 + 2, assert revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateSupplyV2 + estimateReserveV2] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER performing a buy-sell round trip with a 17-decimal quote token via swapExactInput on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses a 17-decimal quote token (scaleFactor=10), curve with CURVE_2 parameters, and circulatingSupply=0 trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) buy with inputAmount=1 unit of 17-decimal token (scaledReserve=10), (2) sell all received tokens, (3) measure quote token balance change, violating INVARIANT: quote_returned <= quote_paid (no round-trip profit from rounding with scaleFactor=10), causing scoped impact: 1 extra unit of 17-decimal quote token returned per sell when scaledReserve in (0, 10)? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test with 17-decimal token, buy 1 unit, sell all tokens, assert quote balance of attacker does not increase.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateSupply] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER buying tokens with a zero-value input on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses CURVE_2 (r=2 ether), and attacker calls swapExactInput with inputAmount=0 trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) call swapExactInput with inputAmount=0, (2) observe if estimateSupply(r + 0) - estimateSupply(r) = 0 (no tokens minted), (3) check if any fee is charged on a zero-value buy, violating INVARIANT: a zero-value buy must result in zero tokens minted and zero BNB transferred, causing scoped impact: if zero-value buy mints tokens (due to rounding in divWadUp), attacker gets free tokens? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test with inputAmount=0, assert supply_delta == 0 and no BNB transferred.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateReserve] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER selling zero tokens on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses CURVE_2 (r=2 ether), and attacker calls swapExactInput with inputAmount=0 (selling 0 tokens) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) call swapExactInput with inputToken=token, outputToken=address(0), inputAmount=0, (2) observe if estimateReserve(supply) - estimateReserve(supply - 0) = 0 (no BNB returned), (3) check if any fee is charged, violating INVARIANT: a zero-token sell must result in zero BNB returned and zero tokens burned, causing scoped impact: if zero-token sell returns BNB (due to rounding), attacker extracts BNB for free? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test with inputAmount=0 sell, assert eth_returned == 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateSupply + estimateReserve] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploiting the monotonicity invariant of estimateSupply on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses any curve type, and attacker performs two buys with eth_in_1 < eth_in_2 trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) buy with eth_in_1, record supply_1, (2) buy with eth_in_2 from the same initial state, record supply_2, (3) assert supply_1 < supply_2, violating INVARIANT: estimateSupply must be strictly monotonically increasing in reserve (larger reserve => larger supply), causing scoped impact: if monotonicity is violated, attacker could buy more tokens with less BNB by choosing a specific input amount? Local proof idea: add Foundry fuzz test with uint256 eth1 < eth2, assert estimateSupply(curve, r + eth1) < estimateSupply(curve, r + eth2).

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateReserve] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploiting the monotonicity invariant of estimateReserve on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses any curve type, and attacker sells two different amounts trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) sell supply_1 tokens, record reserve_1 = estimateReserve(current_supply - supply_1), (2) sell supply_2 > supply_1 tokens from the same state, record reserve_2 = estimateReserve(current_supply - supply_2), (3) assert reserve_2 < reserve_1 (more tokens sold => lower remaining reserve => more BNB returned), violating INVARIANT: estimateReserve must be strictly monotonically decreasing in supply (lower supply => lower reserve), causing scoped impact: if monotonicity is violated, attacker could sell fewer tokens and receive more BNB? Local proof idea: add Foundry fuzz test with uint256 s1 < s2 <= TOTAL_SUPPLY, assert estimateReserve(curve, s1) < estimateReserve(curve, s2).

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateSupplyV2] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER buying tokens with a 6-decimal quote token and a large reserve on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses CURVE_RH_USD (r=3837 ether, h=107036752 ether, k=4247700017424 ether) with 6-decimal USDC, and attacker sends inputAmount = type(uint128).max (max stored reserve) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) call swapExactInput with inputAmount = type(uint128).max = ~3.4e38 USDC units, (2) scaledReserve = 3.4e38 * 1e12 = 3.4e50, (3) check if r + scaledReserve overflows uint256 (3.4e50 + 3837e18 ≈ 3.4e50 < 1.15e77, no overflow), (4) check if divWadUp(k, r + scaledReserve) is near 0, causing supply ≈ TOTAL_SUPPLY + h, violating INVARIANT: supply must not exceed TOTAL_SUPPLY, causing scoped impact: if supply exceeds TOTAL_SUPPLY, attacker gets more tokens than the total supply? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test with inputAmount = type(uint128).max, assert supply <= TOTAL_SUPPLY.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateReserveV2] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploiting the rounding direction mismatch between estimateSupplyV2 and estimateReserveV2 for non-18 decimal tokens on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses a 6-decimal quote token, curve with CURVE_RH_USD, and circulatingSupply=0 trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) buy with inputAmount=X USDC units, receive supply_delta tokens, (2) sell supply_delta tokens, receive Y USDC units, (3) assert Y <= X, violating INVARIANT: for any buy-sell round trip, quote_returned <= quote_paid (no profit from rounding direction mismatch between estimateSupplyV2 and estimateReserveV2), causing scoped impact: USDC extraction if the double round-up in estimateReserveV2 exceeds the round-down in estimateSupplyV2? Local proof idea: add Foundry fuzz test with uint256 X in [1, 1e12], assert Y <= X for all values.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateSupply] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER buying tokens when the curve has h > 0 and the initial supply is non-zero on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses CURVE_RH_TOSHI (r=6140351 ether, h=107036752 ether, k=6797594227179952 ether), and circulatingSupply=0 (fresh token), but estimateSupply(r) = TOTAL_SUPPLY + h - divWadUp(k, r + r) may not equal 0 trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) call estimateSupply(curve, r) to find the supply at reserve=r (the virtual reserve), (2) check if this equals 0 (expected for a fresh token), (3) if not, the curve starts with a non-zero supply that was never minted, violating INVARIANT: estimateSupply(curve, r) must equal 0 for a fresh token (no supply at the virtual reserve), causing scoped impact: if estimateSupply(r) > 0, the curve overestimates supply, allowing attacker to buy tokens at a lower price than intended? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test computing estimateSupply(curve_rh_toshi, r) and assert result == 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.price] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploiting the price() function returning type(uint256).max to bypass DEX migration threshold checks on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses a curve where supply approaches TOTAL_SUPPLY + h - 1e9 - 1, and any downstream code uses price() to determine if migration should occur trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) buy tokens until price() returns type(uint256).max, (2) observe if any migration or swap function treats type(uint256).max as a valid price and proceeds with incorrect calculations, (3) check if migration uses the sentinel price to compute LP amounts, violating INVARIANT: price() must not return a sentinel value that causes incorrect LP amount calculations during migration, causing scoped impact: BNB or LP token extraction if migration uses type(uint256).max as a price? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test buying tokens to the sentinel threshold, then triggering migration, assert migration uses correct reserve amounts.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/Curve.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets an unprivileged user gain, buy, sell, swap, launch, migrate, claim, recover, withdraw, redeem, or redirect more BNB, quote tokens, launched tokens, LP fees, tax funds, dividend funds, salt-lock fees, or other protocol/user value than intended'] [Function: LibCurve.estimateReserve + PortalBase._burnToken] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploiting the circulatingSupply underflow in _burnToken on proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 under PRECONDITIONS: token uses TOKEN_V2_PERMIT, and attacker sells more tokens than circulatingSupply (e.g. by transferring tokens from another address to Portal before selling) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: (1) transfer tokens to Portal, (2) call swapExactInput to sell, (3) observe if state.circulatingSupply -= uint128(amount) underflows when amount > circulatingSupply, violating INVARIANT: circulatingSupply must never underflow below 0, causing scoped impact: if circulatingSupply underflows to near type(uint128).max, estimateReserve(type(uint128).max) returns 0, and subsequent sellers receive the entire reserve? Local proof idea: add Foundry unit test attempting to sell more tokens than circulatingSupply, assert revert.
